Title: Learn to Code in Python
ShortDescription: Start coding in Python with hands-on lessons that build the habits you'll use in every future course.
MetaDescription: Learn Python from scratch by writing real code. Practice variables, loops, functions, data structures, and problem solving in an interactive course.
Description: Learn the basics of the Python programming language, and why it's one of the most popular programming languages out there. You'll get hands-on practice with all the core concepts, and learn how to apply them to real-world problems. You don't need any prior programming experience to be able to complete this course, it's intended for complete beginners.

Chapters.Title: Introduction
Chapters.Description: Learn about the Python programming language and why it's so ubiquitous

Chapters.Title: Variables
Chapters.Description: Learn how variables are declared and used in Python

Chapters.Title: Functions
Chapters.Description: Learn how to declare and call functions

Chapters.Title: Scope
Chapters.Description: Learn how Python scopes variables in different contexts

Chapters.Title: Testing and Debugging
Chapters.Description: Learn how unit tests and good debugging practices will help you write better code

Chapters.Title: Computing
Chapters.Description: Learn the basics of how computers work internally

Chapters.Title: Comparisons
Chapters.Description: Learn how to compare different values and make logical decisions in a program

Chapters.Title: Loops
Chapters.Description: Master the art of reusing code

Chapters.Title: Lists
Chapters.Description: Learn the most basic and powerful data structure in all of coding

Chapters.Title: Dictionaries
Chapters.Description: Learn about Python's built-in support for complex structures

Chapters.Title: Sets
Chapters.Description: Master another basic data structure of the Python language

Chapters.Title: Errors
Chapters.Description: Learn how to deal with unexpected data and inputs in a Python program

Chapters.Title: Type Hints
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use type hints to make Python code easier to read and debug

Chapters.Title: Practice
Chapters.Description: Put your skills to work by squashing extra challenging practice problems

Chapters.Title: Quiz
Chapters.Description: Quiz yourself on some of the important-to-know facts about the Python language

Title: Learn Linux
ShortDescription: Never fear a Linux terminal again. Navigate the filesystem, inspect processes, and automate everyday developer tasks.
MetaDescription: Learn the Linux command line fundamentals. Navigate files, manage processes, use pipes, inspect systems, and automate terminal tasks.
Description: So many developers go years before understanding the basics of shells, terminals, and Linux (or more generally Unix-like systems). Here you'll learn to navigate files and directories, install dependencies, configure your PATH, and use powerful CLI tools like grep, find, and pipes. Never fear the command line again, make it fear you.

Chapters.Title: The Command Line
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use a CLI, and why text-based interfaces are so powerful.

Chapters.Title: Filesystems
Chapters.Description: Learn how to navigate a filesystem, and how to create, move, and delete files and directories.

Chapters.Title: Programs
Chapters.Description: Practice the ins and outs of executing programs and scripts from the command line and understand your PATH

Chapters.Title: Input/Output
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use command line flags, arguments, and connect the stdin and stdout of programs to create modular automations

Chapters.Title: Local CLI
Chapters.Description: Move onto your own machine and cover everything the browser couldn't: a real terminal and WSL, manual pages, interactive pagers, live processes, and real users, root, and sudo.

Chapters.Title: Permissions
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use permissions to control access to files and directories.

Chapters.Title: Editors and Packages
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use package managers like Homebrew, APT and Webi to install and manage software, and set up your development environment.

Title: Learn Go
ShortDescription: Learn Go from the ground up: all the simple syntax, explicit errors, interfaces, and patterns you'll use to build real services.
MetaDescription: Learn the Go programming language from start to finish. Practice structs, interfaces, errors, HTTP, concurrency, and idiomatic patterns by writing real Go code.
Description: Master the fundamentals of the Go programming language in this comprehensive interactive course. Go is one of the most popular languages of 2025, and Go developers are among the highest paid in the world. You'll practice writing performant, idiomatic Go with these hands-on lessons, challenges and quizzes.

Chapters.Title: Variables
Chapters.Description: Learn the basic syntax for declaring and using variables and why you should use Go in the first place

Chapters.Title: Constants and Formatting
Chapters.Description: Learn how constants work in Go and how to format output using the fmt and unicode packages.

Chapters.Title: Conditionals
Chapters.Description: Use if/else statements to control the flow of your program

Chapters.Title: Functions
Chapters.Description: Learn about how functions behave

Chapters.Title: Structs
Chapters.Description: Idiomatically model data in Go using structs

Chapters.Title: Interfaces
Chapters.Description: Master one of Go's most powerful tools: the interface

Chapters.Title: Errors
Chapters.Description: Learn how Go uniquely handles errors

Chapters.Title: Loops
Chapters.Description: Practice various algorithms in Go

Chapters.Title: Slices
Chapters.Description: Learn about Go's take on ordered lists

Chapters.Title: Maps
Chapters.Description: Go has an answer to Python's sets and dictionaries, we call it a map

Chapters.Title: Pointers
Chapters.Description: Learn about pointers and their pitfalls

Chapters.Title: Packages and Modules
Chapters.Description: Learn how go projects are organized on your local machine

Chapters.Title: Channels
Chapters.Description: Master the #1 reason to use Go: its elegance of concurrent programming

Chapters.Title: Mutexes
Chapters.Description: Continue learning about concurrency with mutexes

Chapters.Title: Generics
Chapters.Description: Learn how Go approaches polymorphism with generics: a new feature added in 1.18

Chapters.Title: Enums
Chapters.Description: Go famously doesn't support proper enums, but we'll go over how Go developers solve the same kinds of problems with the tools they have.

Chapters.Title: Quiz
Chapters.Description: Learn about go-specific design patterns and fun language facts

Title: Learn Git
ShortDescription: Don't be the developer that uses Git to simply add, commit, push, and pray. Learn how it works under the hood.
MetaDescription: Learn Git by practicing commits, branches, merges, rebases, history, and conflict resolution. Start truly understanding version control.
Description: Ah, Git. The source control system that every developer uses, but few truly understand. In this course, we'll not just learn how to use Git, but we'll go over some of the plumbing commands that make it all work. By the end, you'll have everything you need to know to get started using Git as a professional developer, plus a bit extra.

Chapters.Title: Setup
Chapters.Description: Install and configure Git on your local machine

Chapters.Title: Repositories
Chapters.Description: Learn about Git repositories, what they are, and how to work with commits

Chapters.Title: Internals
Chapters.Description: Learn how Git stores data on the files system and the plumbing commands that make it all work

Chapters.Title: Config
Chapters.Description: Learn how to configure Git and set up your user information

Chapters.Title: Branching
Chapters.Description: Practice creating and switching between branches

Chapters.Title: Merge
Chapters.Description: Merge changes from one branch into another and learn how merge commits work

Chapters.Title: Rebase
Chapters.Description: Learn about the cooler way to integrate changes from one branch into another

Chapters.Title: Reset
Chapters.Description: Learn how to undo changes with the reset command

Chapters.Title: Remote
Chapters.Description: Setup a remote repository and learn how to push and pull changes

Chapters.Title: GitHub
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use Git with GitHub, the most popular Git hosting service

Chapters.Title: Gitignore
Chapters.Description: Learn about using a .gitignore file to exclude files and directories from being tracked by Git.

Title: Learn Object Oriented Programming in Python
ShortDescription: Use classes when they help, skip the complexity when they don't, and learn OOP as a practical way to organize code.
MetaDescription: Learn object-oriented programming in Python by building classes, methods, inheritance, encapsulation, and reusable program structures.
Description: Learn the fundamentals of the most widely-used programming paradigm today: object-oriented programming. You will model real-world problems within your programs, and learn how to write code that is easy to understand and maintain. By the end of this course you'll be writing production-quality Python code.

Chapters.Title: Clean Code
Chapters.Description: Understand why we have programming paradigms in the first place, and how they help us write more maintainable code

Chapters.Title: Classes
Chapters.Description: Learn about classes and objects, and how OOP differs from a functional programming paradigm.

Chapters.Title: Encapsulation
Chapters.Description: Learn how to write cleaner code by encapsulating data and methods.

Chapters.Title: Abstraction
Chapters.Description: Abstraction is a powerful tool that will make your code more robust and reusable.

Chapters.Title: Inheritance
Chapters.Description: The crux of OOP is the ability to inherit data and behaviors from other classes

Chapters.Title: Polymorphism
Chapters.Description: Polymorphism is one of the best ideas we've had as developers, learn how it works.

Title: Build a Bookbot in Python
ShortDescription: Build your first local command-line app and use the real tools developers use every day.
MetaDescription: Build a text analysis engine in Python as your first simple project. Read files, count words and characters, and practice Git and developer workflows.
Description: You'll build a real command-line application in Python that does data analysis on text files, or rather, entire novels like "Frankenstein", "Moby Dick" or "Pride and Prejudice". You'll be guided through the set up of a professional development environment using Python. This is the perfect first Python project for beginners.

Chapters.Title: Setup
Chapters.Description: Set up a local Python development environment

Chapters.Title: Data Analysis
Chapters.Description: Write Python functions that analyze data from a novel of text and extrapolate word and character counts

Chapters.Title: Report
Chapters.Description: Build a beautiful analytics report and add command-line tooling to make your application usable with any plain text file

Title: Learn JavaScript
ShortDescription: Get comfortable with JavaScript by writing code, solving exercises, and learning the language TypeScript builds on.
MetaDescription: Learn JavaScript from start to finish with hands-on exercises. Practice functions, objects, arrays, control flow, and the browser language behind the web.
Description: Master the fundamentals of modern JavaScript in this comprehensive interactive course. You'll be writing real-world code in minutes and solving challenges to cement your understanding of JS concepts. Learn how to use the most up-to-date JavaScript features in bite-sized hands on lessons, challenges and quizzes.

Chapters.Title: Variables
Chapters.Description: Learn the basic syntax of JavaScript and how to declare variables

Chapters.Title: Comparisons
Chapters.Description: Learn how to compare values and write conditional statements

Chapters.Title: Functions
Chapters.Description: Learn about the different ways to create and execute functions in JavaScript

Chapters.Title: Objects
Chapters.Description: Objects are one of the most-used JS structures, learn to master them

Chapters.Title: Classes
Chapters.Description: Learn about classes in JavaScript and how they differ from POJOs and the classes in other languages

Chapters.Title: Prototypes
Chapters.Description: Explore JavaScript prototypes and how they enable inheritance without traditional classes.

Chapters.Title: Loops
Chapters.Description: Understand and practice the many ways to iterate over a list of data

Chapters.Title: Arrays
Chapters.Description: Learn about contiguous memory storage in JavaScript

Chapters.Title: Errors
Chapters.Description: Practice good error handling with JavaScripts try/catch

Chapters.Title: Sets
Chapters.Description: Practice using the new ES6 (ES2015) set object for collections of unique values

Chapters.Title: Maps
Chapters.Description: Learn about the new ES6 map object for collections of key-value pairs

Chapters.Title: Promises
Chapters.Description: Understand promises and asynchronous programming in JavaScript, as well as the new async/await syntax

Chapters.Title: The Event Loop
Chapters.Description: Grok one of the most important elusive concepts beknownst to JavaScript developers, the asynchronous event loop

Chapters.Title: Runtimes
Chapters.Description: Learn about the different JavaScript runtimes and how they differ

Chapters.Title: Modules
Chapters.Description: ES6 Modules help us organize our code into separate files. Learn how to use them and how they differ from CommonJS.

Title: Learn SQL
ShortDescription: Get dangerous with relational databases: query, filter, join, and aggregate data without hiding behind an ORM.
MetaDescription: Learn SQL by querying real relational data. Practice SELECT, joins, filters, aggregations, table design, and database fundamentals.
Description: Learn all the basics of Structured Query Language in this comprehensive SQL course. You'll build out real database tables and practice querying them in flexible ways right in the browser. Not only will you understand how to use SQL, but you'll also learn in what situations you should use it. We will cover architectural design patterns and how to use SQL in a production environment.

Chapters.Title: Introduction
Chapters.Description: Learn about the basics of SQL and how it came to be

Chapters.Title: Tables
Chapters.Description: Create new tables and learn how to structure data efficiently

Chapters.Title: Constraints
Chapters.Description: Learn how to keep data accurate and up to date

Chapters.Title: CRUD
Chapters.Description: Create, read, update and delete information from a SQL database

Chapters.Title: Basic Queries
Chapters.Description: Practice additional clauses and features that SQL offers for more flexible querying

Chapters.Title: Structuring
Chapters.Description: Learn how to order and limit the data returned in large query sets

Chapters.Title: Aggregations
Chapters.Description: Learn how to run powerful calculations on entire datasets

Chapters.Title: Subqueries
Chapters.Description: Practice the advanced technique of nesting queries inside one another

Chapters.Title: Normalization
Chapters.Description: Learn how to normalize a database in order to keep it's data consistent and accurate

Chapters.Title: Joins
Chapters.Description: Practice joining multiple tables together in order to power real world usecases

Chapters.Title: Performance
Chapters.Description: Learn how to keep databases running fast and efficient in production

Title: Learn Data Structures and Algorithms in Python
ShortDescription: Build the data structures yourself, then use them to solve harder problems with less guessing.
MetaDescription: Learn data structures and algorithms in Python by building stacks, queues, trees, graphs, sorting, searching, and Big O intuition from scratch.
Description: If you've had trouble getting past a hard whiteboarding session, this course is for you. Big-O complexity is arguably the most important concept students learn in a formal computer science degree. You'll build data structures from scratch in Python and improve your problem-solving skills. We'll cover binary trees, linked lists, stacks, graphs and more. This Python course will give you the foundation you need to start your career off on the right foot. After completing this course, you'll be comfortable crushing interview questions and writing performant code.

Chapters.Title: Algorithms Intro
Chapters.Description: Learn about what algorithms are and why they matter

Chapters.Title: Math
Chapters.Description: Learn the math required to understand Big-O notation, namely exponents, logarithms, and factorials

Chapters.Title: Big-O Analysis
Chapters.Description: Figure out what Big-O notation and time complexity mean in the context of algorithms and performant code

Chapters.Title: Sorting Algorithms
Chapters.Description: Learn how data is sorted on a computer, and how to sort it faster

Chapters.Title: Exponential Time
Chapters.Description: Understand why exponential time complexity is so dangerous

Chapters.Title: Data Structures Intro
Chapters.Description: Learn about data structures and how they play a critical role in algorithms

Chapters.Title: Stacks
Chapters.Description: Learn about stacks, the original LIFO data structure and build one from scratch

Chapters.Title: Queues
Chapters.Description: Learn about the FIFO queue data structure and how to implement a simple one from scratch

Chapters.Title: Linked Lists
Chapters.Description: Understand how linked lists vary from arrays and how to use one to build a faster queue

Chapters.Title: Binary Trees
Chapters.Description: Learn about binary trees, what they are used for, and implement one from scratch

Chapters.Title: Red Black Trees
Chapters.Description: Solve the classic balancing problem of traditional binary trees with a red-black algorithm

Chapters.Title: Hashmaps
Chapters.Description: Build a hashmap from scratch and learn how to use the Python dictionary type effectively

Chapters.Title: Tries
Chapters.Description: Build all the methods of a trie class, and efficiently search entire documents of text

Chapters.Title: Graphs
Chapters.Description: Learn about graph structures and how we can use them to quickly solve a wide array of search problems

Chapters.Title: BFS and DFS
Chapters.Description: Implement and understand the ever-famous breadth first and depth-first search algorithms

Chapters.Title: P vs NP
Chapters.Description: Learn about P and NP

Title: Learn Functional Programming in Python
ShortDescription: Add functional programming to your toolbox without pretending Python is Haskell.
MetaDescription: Learn functional programming in Python with pure functions, immutability, recursion, higher-order functions, closures, and practical FP patterns.
Description: Functional programming is back in vogue. There's a reason front-end frameworks like React and Vue are moving toward functional programming, and after this course you'll more than understand its benefits. We'll dive in-depth into all of the foundational concepts of functional programming in a familiar language.

Chapters.Title: What is Functional Programming?
Chapters.Description: Learn about the difference between OOP and functional styles, and what the real difference is: imperative vs. declarative

Chapters.Title: First-Class Functions
Chapters.Description: First-class and higher-order functions are foundational to everything we do in the functional paradigm.

Chapters.Title: Pure Functions
Chapters.Description: My favorite part of functional programming is the focus on pure functions that are easy to test and reason about

Chapters.Title: Recursion
Chapters.Description: While not unique to functional programming, recursion is a powerful tool that is used often when trying to avoid imperative code

Chapters.Title: Function Transformations
Chapters.Description: Learn how to transform functions into other functions to write more abstract and generalized code

Chapters.Title: Closures
Chapters.Description: Learn about encapsulating state within functions and how to use closures to write more expressive code

Chapters.Title: Currying
Chapters.Description: Learn to transform a function with multiple parameters into a series of functions with one parameter, and why you might want to do that

Chapters.Title: Decorators
Chapters.Description: Learn about Python's decorators and how they're essentially syntactic sugar for higher-order functions

Chapters.Title: Sum Types
Chapters.Description: Learn about sum types, a powerful way to represent data that is used in many functional languages

Title: Learn Memory Management in C
ShortDescription: Get close to the metal: pointers, the stack, the heap, and a tiny garbage collector you build yourself.
MetaDescription: Learn memory management in C by working with pointers, stack and heap allocation, malloc, free, and a garbage collector you build yourself.
Description: Memory management is something that every programmer needs to deal with and understand at some point. If you're used to working with languages and tools that handle it for you, this course will teach you how all that works under the hood. You'll even build your own garbage collectors from scratch. This course will teach you the C basics you need for the course, but it's not a course about C, it's about memory.

Chapters.Title: C Basics
Chapters.Description: Introduction to the basics of the C programming language

Chapters.Title: Structs
Chapters.Description: Understand how C structs work and how they're laid out in memory

Chapters.Title: Pointers
Chapters.Description: You thought pointers were hard? Wrong. Let's figure them out together.

Chapters.Title: Enums
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use enums (enumerations) in C to create a set of named constants.

Chapters.Title: Unions
Chapters.Description: Unions in C are not the same as they are in TypeScript. Let's grok C unions.

Chapters.Title: Stack and Heap
Chapters.Description: Understand the difference between allocating memory on the stack and the heap

Chapters.Title: Advanced Pointers
Chapters.Description: Let's practice some more advanced pointer concepts - like pointers to pointers

Chapters.Title: Stack Data Structure
Chapters.Description: Build a stack in C using structs and pointers and understand where the memory is allocated

Chapters.Title: Objects
Chapters.Description: Implement your own object system for later use in your garbage collection algorithms

Chapters.Title: Refcounting GC
Chapters.Description: Implement a simple reference counting garbage collector from scratch in C

Chapters.Title: Mark and Sweep GC
Chapters.Description: Implement a basic mark and sweep garbage collector and understand its tradeoffs with refcounting

Title: Build Asteroids using Python and Pygame
ShortDescription: Make a real arcade game while practicing OOP, vectors, collision detection, and game loops.
MetaDescription: Build Asteroids in Python with Pygame. Practice game loops, sprites, collision detection, vectors, and object-oriented design.
Description: Build a clone of the classic Asteroids game using Pygame and object-oriented programming concepts. This guided project will help you understand how to use Pygame to create a game loop, handle user input, and manage game state. You'll also learn how to use object-oriented programming to create game objects and manage their interactions.

Chapters.Title: Pygame
Chapters.Description: Setup and install Pygame.

Chapters.Title: Gameloop
Chapters.Description: Write a gameloop that renders the game at 60 frames per second.

Chapters.Title: Player
Chapters.Description: Create a Player class and implement movement.

Chapters.Title: Asteroids
Chapters.Description: Add asteroids for the player to avoid.

Title: Learn Docker
ShortDescription: Understand Docker by actually using it: images, containers, volumes, networks, and why teams ship with it.
MetaDescription: Learn Docker by building images, running containers, wiring networks, mounting volumes, and packaging apps for local dev and deployment.
Description: Learn Docker from the ground up by installing, running, creating, and publishing Docker containers. Take your Back-end and DevOps skills to the next level. You will discover how Docker fits into production back-end architectures and complete hands-on projects on your local machine. This course is jam-packed with video explanations and rich text explanations so you'll never get lost.

Chapters.Title: Install
Chapters.Description: Get Docker & Docker Hub installed on your local development machine

Chapters.Title: Containers
Chapters.Description: Learn about containers and images and how to start and stop them

Chapters.Title: Storage
Chapters.Description: Practice creating Docker volumes and deploying a blog using the Ghost's official Docker image

Chapters.Title: Execute
Chapters.Description: Learn how to execute arbitrary commands and start shell sessions in containers

Chapters.Title: Networks
Chapters.Description: Learn how to connect Docker containers by using bridge networks

Chapters.Title: Dockerfiles
Chapters.Description: Deploy custom applications on Docker by building your own Docker images

Chapters.Title: Debug
Chapters.Description: Learn how to debug and troubleshoot Docker containers

Chapters.Title: Publish
Chapters.Description: Publish your own images up to Docker Hub

Title: Build an AI Agent in Python
ShortDescription: Build a tiny coding agent that can read files, edit code, run Python, and loop on feedback.
MetaDescription: Build an AI coding agent in Python with OpenRouter, function calling, file editing, code execution, and feedback loops.
Description: Write a toy agentic code editor in Python, similar to Claude Code or Cursor's Agent Mode. Understand how agents work from scratch by using free models on OpenRouter (via the OpenAI SDK) to create an LLM-powered code agent. You'll use function calling and feedback loops to build an agent that can find and fix bugs in a real project!

Chapters.Title: LLMs
Chapters.Description: Learn how LLMs work and send prompts to LLMs on OpenRouter using the OpenAI Python SDK

Chapters.Title: Functions
Chapters.Description: Write the functions your AI agent will need to modify files and run Python code

Chapters.Title: Function Calling
Chapters.Description: Give your AI agent the ability to call functions based on its system prompt and context window

Chapters.Title: Agents
Chapters.Description: Give your tool a proper feedback loop to make it truly "agentic" and fix a real bug

Title: Learn HTTP Clients in TypeScript
ShortDescription: Call real APIs, parse JSON, handle errors, and make HTTP feel boring in TypeScript.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP clients in TypeScript by making API requests, parsing JSON, handling errors, authentication, and working with real web data.
Description: Learn the backbone of the modern web: HTTP network communications. You will be writing real HTTP requests in TypeScript, then parsing and displaying the responses. Each challenge in this course is part of a larger application so that you will understand how HTTP is used in the real world.

Chapters.Title: Why HTTP?
Chapters.Description: Learn about HTTP and how it powers the majority of web communication

Chapters.Title: DNS
Chapters.Description: Learn about domain names and how they map human-readable names to physical servers

Chapters.Title: URIs
Chapters.Description: Understand URIs and URLs at a deeper level

Chapters.Title: Errors
Chapters.Description: Learn how to handle errors in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Headers
Chapters.Description: Practice adding custom headers to HTTP requests and learn about authorization

Chapters.Title: JSON
Chapters.Description: Learn one of the most popular data formatting notations in the programming world

Chapters.Title: Methods
Chapters.Description: Learn about GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests

Chapters.Title: Paths
Chapters.Description: Learn how URL paths and query parameters relate to typical HTTP requests

Chapters.Title: HTTPS
Chapters.Description: Learn how to encrypt HTTP requests and keep them secure

Chapters.Title: Runtime Validation
Chapters.Description: Learn runtime validation for HTTP responses using Zod to ensure type safety when working with external APIs

Title: Build a Static Site Generator in Python
ShortDescription: Build the kind of tool that turns Markdown into a website, then understand static site generators from the inside.
MetaDescription: Build a static site generator in Python. Parse Markdown, generate HTML, copy assets, and learn how publishing tools work from the inside.
Description: Ever wondered how SEO and performance-optimized static site generators like Hugo work? In this guided project you'll build your own from scratch using Python. You'll put a lot of your learnings from Object-Oriented Programming and Functional Programming to use in a tangible web project. This project isn't for the faint of heart, but it's well worth the effort. You'll come away with a deeper understanding of static content management.

Chapters.Title: Static Sites
Chapters.Description: Learn about what a static site is, and start building the functionality necessary to process and move static HTML and Markdown files.

Chapters.Title: Nodes
Chapters.Description: Build the core HTML generation logic that will power your static site generator. Use recursion and OOP to build an easily understandable and maintainable system.

Chapters.Title: Inline
Chapters.Description: Build the inline markdown parsing logic, and the logic to generate inline HTML elements.

Chapters.Title: Blocks
Chapters.Description: Handle entire blocks of markdown, and generate the HTML nodes that represent them.

Chapters.Title: Website
Chapters.Description: Put the entire static site generator together, and publish your first website.

Title: Learn TypeScript
ShortDescription: Turn your shaky JavaScript into type safe code with a deep understanding of TypeScript.
MetaDescription: Learn TypeScript the right way. Practice types, interfaces, unions, generics, narrowing, and utility types in hands-on exercises.
Description: Master TypeScript from start to finish in this immersive, hands-on course. TypeScript's meteoric rise in popularity, both on the frontend and backend, has brought demand for TypeScript developers to among the highest in the industry. In this course you'll learn all the fundamentals of the language while solving real-world type challenges each step of the way.

Chapters.Title: Types
Chapters.Description: Learn the basic types in TypeScript and the syntax for using them

Chapters.Title: Functions
Chapters.Description: Learn how to write type-safe functions with TypeScript's robust type system.

Chapters.Title: Unions
Chapters.Description: Learn how to declare and use union types in TypeScript to allow variables to hold multiple types

Chapters.Title: Arrays
Chapters.Description: Learn to work with typed arrays in TypeScript, including syntax, generics, unions and type inference

Chapters.Title: Objects
Chapters.Description: Learn how to work with object types in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Tuples
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use tuples in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Intersections
Chapters.Description: Learn the basics of intersections in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Interfaces
Chapters.Description: Discover the core principles of interfaces in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Enums
Chapters.Description: Practice using enums in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Type Narrowing
Chapters.Description: Learn how TypeScript uses type narrowing to infer more about your code.

Chapters.Title: Classes
Chapters.Description: Learn about TypeScript's class features, including class syntax, access modifiers, inheritance, interfaces, and more.

Chapters.Title: Utility Types
Chapters.Description: Learn about some utility types that can come in handy in TypeScript.

Chapters.Title: Generics
Chapters.Description: Learn how to create reusable components with type parameters in TypeScript

Chapters.Title: Conditional Types
Chapters.Description: Learn how to create types that react dynamically to other types using TypeScript's powerful conditional type system.

Chapters.Title: Local Development
Chapters.Description: Learn how to set up and optimize your TypeScript development environment.

Title: Learn Kubernetes
ShortDescription: Deploy real services on Kubernetes and learn the orchestrator without pretending the YAML is the interesting part.
MetaDescription: Learn Kubernetes by deploying real services on a local cluster. Practice pods, deployments, services, config, scaling, and debugging.
Description: Learn the fundamentals of the most popular container orchestrator in the world: Kubernetes. You'll get your hands dirty and deploy Kubernetes applications, pods, services and even the Gateway API. This isn't just a tutorial, you'll set up real systems, but we'll also cover the theory, concepts and use cases of the technology.

Chapters.Title: Install
Chapters.Description: This chapter is a guide through the initial setup and installation of Kubernetes tools. Learn to install and configure kubectl and minikube, setting the foundation for your Kubernetes journey.

Chapters.Title: Pods
Chapters.Description: Explore the fundamental building blocks of Kubernetes: Pods. Understand their lifecycle, how to create and manage them, and their role in a Kubernetes environment.

Chapters.Title: Deployments
Chapters.Description: Learn about Deployments and how they allow you to manage and update your applications with ease. Learn about infrastructure as code.

Chapters.Title: ConfigMaps
Chapters.Description: Uncover the power of ConfigMaps in Kubernetes for managing configuration data. Learn to create, deploy, and update ConfigMaps to externalize and manage application configurations.

Chapters.Title: Services
Chapters.Description: Learn about Kubernetes Services, an essential feature for app communication. Discover different service types, how they route traffic, and their role in microservices architecture.

Chapters.Title: Gateway
Chapters.Description: Dive into Gateway in Kubernetes, understanding how to expose, manage, and secure HTTP/HTTPS routes to services. 		Learn about Gateway API resources and different Gateway types.

Chapters.Title: Storage
Chapters.Description: Understand Kubernetes' approach to persistent storage. Learn about persistent volumes, claims, and the configuration of storage options for stateful applications.

Chapters.Title: Namespaces
Chapters.Description: Discover how to organize your Kubernetes clusters using namespaces. Learn to isolate resources, manage permissions, and simplify cluster management.

Chapters.Title: Scaling
Chapters.Description: Master the art of scaling in Kubernetes, both vertically and horizontally. Understand the metrics, resource limits, and auto-scaling capabilities to ensure optimal performance.

Chapters.Title: Nodes
Chapters.Description: Get to grips with the role of nodes in Kubernetes. Learn about different node types, resource allocation, and how to manage and maintain node health for a cluster.

Title: Learn HTTP Clients in Go
ShortDescription: Call real APIs, parse JSON, handle errors, and make HTTP feel boring in Go.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP clients in Go by making API requests, parsing JSON, handling errors, authentication, and working with real web data.
Description: Learn the backbone of the modern web: HTTP network communications. You will be writing real HTTP requests in Go, then parsing and displaying the responses. Each challenge in this course is part of a larger application so that you will understand how HTTP is used in the real world.

Chapters.Title: Why HTTP?
Chapters.Description: Learn about HTTP and how it powers the majority of web communication

Chapters.Title: JSON
Chapters.Description: Learn one of the most popular data formatting notations in the programming world

Chapters.Title: DNS
Chapters.Description: Learn about domain names and how they map human-readable names to physical servers

Chapters.Title: URIs
Chapters.Description: Understand URIs and URLs at a deeper level

Chapters.Title: Headers
Chapters.Description: Practice adding custom headers to HTTP requests and learn about authorization

Chapters.Title: Methods
Chapters.Description: Learn about GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests

Chapters.Title: Paths
Chapters.Description: Learn how URL paths and query parameters relate to typical HTTP requests

Chapters.Title: HTTPS
Chapters.Description: Learn how to encrypt HTTP requests and keep them secure

Chapters.Title: Errors
Chapters.Description: Learn how to handle errors in Go

Chapters.Title: cURL
Chapters.Description: Use cURL and jq to make HTTP requests from the command line

Title: First Personal Project
ShortDescription: Pick an idea, scope it down, build it yourself, and get community feedback on the result.
MetaDescription: Build a personal programming project from scratch with Boot.dev guardrails. Scope an idea, ship it, and get community feedback.
Description: You can't truly become a developer if you can't build your own stuff. Theory and foundations are important, but you also need to be able to fly solo. The purpose of this project is two-fold: a chance to put your skills into practice and to add another project to your portfolio. Well-built personal projects will help you land interviews and jobs, so make sure to put in the effort!

Chapters.Title: Placeholder
Chapters.Description: Placeholder

Title: Learn How to Find a Programming Job
GenericTitle: Learn How to Find a Programming Job
ShortDescription: Turn your projects into proof, your resume into a signal, and your job search into something less random.
MetaDescription: Learn how to find a programming job with portfolio strategy, resumes, networking, applications, and interview prep for new developers.
Description: Learn how to find a job as a software engineer. This course will walk you through polishing up your portfolio projects, creating a resume, networking, interviewing, and even thinking about relocation. If you're nervous about starting your job search, this course will get you started on the right foot.

Chapters.Title: Strategy
Chapters.Description: Learn how we think about job searching and how to get your head in the right place

Chapters.Title: Projects
Chapters.Description: Learn how to polish up your portfolio projects and make them stand out

Chapters.Title: GitHub Profile
Chapters.Description: Learn how to get your GitHub profile up to snuff and make it stand out

Chapters.Title: Resume
Chapters.Description: Learn how to write a resume that won't get thrown in the trash

Chapters.Title: LinkedIn Profile
Chapters.Description: Get your LinkedIn profile into a place you're proud of

Chapters.Title: Applying
Chapters.Description: Learn how to apply to jobs and get your resume in front of the right people

Chapters.Title: Networking
Chapters.Description: Start going to local meetups and networking events to meet people in the industry

Chapters.Title: Interviewing
Chapters.Description: Learn about common interviewing pitfalls and how to avoid them

Chapters.Title: Relocation
Chapters.Description: Understand the tradeoffs of relocating for a job and how to make the decision

Title: Learn HTTP Servers in Go
ShortDescription: Build the server side of the web: routes, middleware, auth, and APIs in Go.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP servers in Go by building routes, handlers, middleware, authentication, JSON APIs, and production-style backend patterns.
Description: Learn how to build and HTTP web server from scratch in the Go programming language. You'll build a production ready JSON API using fairly minimalistic tools. You'll incorporate middleware, routing, logging, webhooks, authentication, authorization, JWTs, and more. This course is the coup-de-grace for any aspiring backend developer.

Chapters.Title: Servers
Chapters.Description: We'll cover the basics of web servers, and why Go is such a great language for building performant web servers

Chapters.Title: Routing
Chapters.Description: We'll cover the basics of HTTP routing, and how to build a router using Go's standard library

Chapters.Title: Architecture
Chapters.Description: We'll talk about some different web architectures, and how to choose the right one for your project

Chapters.Title: JSON
Chapters.Description: Learn about how to parse and send JSON data from a Go server

Chapters.Title: Storage
Chapters.Description: We'll add a PostgreSQL database and talk about data storage and database migrations

Chapters.Title: Authentication
Chapters.Description: We'll build an authentication system using JWTs from scratch, and compare JWTs to other authentication methods

Chapters.Title: Authorization
Chapters.Description: Learn about how authorization differs from authentication, and how to implement it in your application

Chapters.Title: Webhooks
Chapters.Description: Webhooks are a special kind of HTTP handler that you'll use often in modern web applications. Learn how to use them here

Chapters.Title: Documentation
Chapters.Description: Learn about documentation, why it's important, and learn about some tools that can help you write good documentation

Title: Build a Pokedex in Go
ShortDescription: Build a command-line Pokedex with a REPL, API calls, JSON parsing, and caching in Go.
MetaDescription: Build a command-line Pokedex in Go with a REPL, HTTP requests, JSON parsing, caching, and API-backed game data.
Description: A REPL, or Read-Eval-Print Loop, is a simple interactive programming environment that takes user input, evaluates it, and returns the result to the user. In this guided project, you'll build a Pokedex-like REPL in Go that uses the PokeAPI to fetch data about Pokemon. It's a great way to put your Go knowledge to the test and learn valuable skills like HTTP networking and data serialization.

Chapters.Title: REPL
Chapters.Description: A REPL (Read, Eval, Print Loop) is a simple interactive programming environment that takes user inputs, evaluates them, and returns the results. Learn how to implement one from scratch in Go.

Chapters.Title: Cache
Chapters.Description: When making network requests, one of the most common performance optimizations is to cache the results. Implement an in-memory cache for the PokeAPI in Go.

Chapters.Title: Pokedex
Chapters.Description: Bring your REPL and cache together to complete your fully functional Pokedex CLI.

Title: Capstone Project
ShortDescription: Build your strongest portfolio project, then turn it into resume and interview material.
MetaDescription: Build a capstone project for your developer portfolio. Scope, implement, debug, ship, and package your work for resumes and interviews.
Description: This capstone project should be your resume's pièce de résistance. You're allowed to use any languages or frameworks you want, but you must build it yourself, and it should be remarkable, memorable even. The purpose of this project is two-fold: a chance to put your skills into practice but also to give your resume something that stands out and gets you noticed.

Chapters.Title: Placeholder
Chapters.Description: Placeholder

Title: Build a Maze Solver in Python
ShortDescription: Draw mazes, solve them, animate the search, and build your algorithms in a visual interface.
MetaDescription: Build a maze solver in Python from scratch. Practice recursion, graph traversal, data structures, and visual algorithmic problem solving.
Description: Use Python and Tkinter to build a GUI that solves mazes. You'll be writing code that draws a randomized maze and then systematically solves it. You will use your knowledge of algorithms to automate this fun game! This is a fantastic way to build another real project and solidify your algorithmic skills.

Chapters.Title: Tkinter
Chapters.Description: Learn how to setup a Python GUI application using Tkinter

Chapters.Title: Cells
Chapters.Description: Draw lines and cells for the maze grid on a graphical canvas

Chapters.Title: Maze
Chapters.Description: Organize cells into a grid and break down the walls into a solvable maze

Chapters.Title: Solve
Chapters.Description: Implement a recursive backtracking algorithm to solve the maze

Title: Learn Pub/Sub in RabbitMQ and Go
ShortDescription: Build event-driven backend flows with RabbitMQ, not just another request-response API.
MetaDescription: Learn Pub/Sub with RabbitMQ and Go. Build producers, consumers, queues, exchanges, and event-driven backend workflows.
Description: Learn how to build large-scale asynchronous Pub/Sub architectures using RabbitMQ and Go. Understand how pub/sub systems differ from point-to-point systems, and how to use real-world technologies to make them work. Write hands-on real-time applications that use a local RabbitMQ server to communicate between services.

Chapters.Title: Pub/Sub Architecture
Chapters.Description: Understand the basics of Pub/Sub architecture and how it differs from point-to-point systems.

Chapters.Title: Message Brokers
Chapters.Description: Understand the role of message brokers in pub/sub systems and which are the most popular options.

Chapters.Title: Publishers & Queues
Chapters.Description: Use publishers and queues to send and receive messages.

Chapters.Title: Subscribers & Routing
Chapters.Description: Learn how to route messages to different subscribers and use advanced routing patterns.

Chapters.Title: Delivery
Chapters.Description: Build robust systems that can handle message delivery failures and ensure messages make it to their destination.

Chapters.Title: Serialization
Chapters.Description: Learn how to serialize and deserialize messages and handle different message formats.

Chapters.Title: Scalability
Chapters.Description: Learn how to handle large-scale systems without sacrificing performance.

Title: Learn CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Docker and Go
ShortDescription: Take a working Go app and automate the boring, breakable parts: tests, builds, containers, and deploys.
MetaDescription: Learn CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Docker for Go apps. Automate tests, builds, containers, and cloud deployments.
Description: You'll build and test a real codebase, and then automate that same build process to deploy an application to the cloud. We'll cover technologies like GitHub Actions, Docker, GCP, Cloud Run, and Turso. If you want to learn how modern tech companies test and ship products, this course is for you.

Chapters.Title: Continuous Integration
Chapters.Description: Learn the basics of CI with GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Tests
Chapters.Description: Learn how to write and run tests both locally and with GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Formatting
Chapters.Description: Learn how to run automated code format checks in the cloud

Chapters.Title: Linting
Chapters.Description: Learn how to run automated code linting checks and learn about the difference between linting and formatting

Chapters.Title: Security
Chapters.Description: Learn about automatic security checks and how to set them up in a Go project

Chapters.Title: Build
Chapters.Description: Learn how to build a Go project in the cloud and Dockerize it in GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Deploy
Chapters.Description: Learn how to deploy a Go project to the public internet using GCP and Cloud Run

Chapters.Title: Database
Chapters.Description: Learn how to connect your public Go service to a managed Turso database. Run migrations in continuous delivery pipelines

Title: Learn the HTTP Protocol in Go
ShortDescription: Build HTTP yourself so requests, responses, headers, and status codes stop feeling like magic.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP from TCP upward in Go. Build an HTTP/1.1 server from scratch and understand requests, responses, headers, and status codes.
Description: The web is built on HTTP, and there's no better way to understand how something works than to implement it yourself. In this course, we'll learn the nitty-gritty details of the HTTP/1.1 protocol, and as we do so we'll actually build our own HTTP server from scratch in Go. By the end, you'll have a deep understanding of how web transport works.

Chapters.Title: HTTP Streams
Chapters.Description: Learn how to read and process a stream of bytes as they arrive.

Chapters.Title: TCP
Chapters.Description: Learn how TCP streams data reliably and how it compares to UDP.

Chapters.Title: Requests
Chapters.Description: Learn how HTTP requests work and how data is sent over the web.

Chapters.Title: Request Lines
Chapters.Description: Learn how to parse HTTP request lines from a stream of data.

Chapters.Title: HTTP Headers
Chapters.Description: Learn how HTTP headers are structured, parsed, and handled.

Chapters.Title: HTTP Body
Chapters.Description: Learn how to read and process the body of an HTTP request.

Chapters.Title: HTTP Responses
Chapters.Description: Learn how an HTTP server constructs and sends responses.

Chapters.Title: Chunked Encoding
Chapters.Description: Learn how HTTP chunked transfer encoding streams data in segments

Chapters.Title: Binary Data
Chapters.Description: Learn how HTTP handles binary data and different protocol versions.

Title: Build a Blog Aggregator in Go
ShortDescription: Build an RSS-powered Go backend with a database, background work, and a real API shape.
MetaDescription: Build a Blog Aggregator in Go with PostgreSQL, RSS feeds, background jobs, and CLI commands for a real backend project.
Description: In this guided project you'll practice building a CLI in Go, and you'll use production-ready database tools like PostgreSQL, SQLc, Goose, and psql. This won't just be another CLI utility, but a service that has a long-running service worker that reaches out over the internet to fetch data from remote locations.

Chapters.Title: Config
Chapters.Description: Build a system for the CLI tool that allows users to get and set configuration values

Chapters.Title: Database
Chapters.Description: Set up Postgres, Goose and SQLC to allow your CLI application to store and retrieve data.

Chapters.Title: RSS
Chapters.Description: Build functions that will download and parse data from RSS feeds

Chapters.Title: Following
Chapters.Description: Add to the multiplayer features of the project by allowing users to follow other RSS feeds

Chapters.Title: Aggregate
Chapters.Description: Turn your CLI into a long running service that continously aggregates posts from RSS feeds.

Title: Learn Git 2
ShortDescription: Move from solo Git to team Git: understand remotes, branches, pull requests, conflicts and worktrees.
MetaDescription: Learn team Git workflows with remotes, pull requests, rebasing, conflicts, and collaboration habits used by professional software teams.
Description: The second installment of our complete Git course. In this advanced course you'll learn how to use Git to collaborate on teams of developers. We will focus a lot more on conflicts and how to safely resolve and revert the natural issues that arise when working on large software teams.

Chapters.Title: Fork
Chapters.Description: Learn how to fork a repository and contribute to open source projects

Chapters.Title: Reflog
Chapters.Description: Work with the reference log to recover lost commits

Chapters.Title: Merge Conflicts
Chapters.Description: Understand merge conflicts, how they arise, and how to resolve them

Chapters.Title: Rebase Conflicts
Chapters.Description: Learn about rebase conflicts and how to safely keep the project history clean

Chapters.Title: Squash
Chapters.Description: Many teams require developers to squash commits, learn how and why you would do it

Chapters.Title: Stash
Chapters.Description: You don't always need to use branches to work on multiple things at once, learn how the stash can save time

Chapters.Title: Revert
Chapters.Description: Git reset is a bit of a blunt tool, learn about git revert and how to safely undo changes

Chapters.Title: Cherry Pick
Chapters.Description: Use git cherry pick to selectively move changes from one branch to another

Chapters.Title: Bisect
Chapters.Description: Slogging through git history can be time consuming, learn how git bisect can find bugs fast

Chapters.Title: Worktrees
Chapters.Description: Learn when git worktrees can be better than regular old branches when it comes to parallel development

Chapters.Title: Tags
Chapters.Description: Learn how to use git tags properly to version and release your code

Title: Learn Cryptography in Go
ShortDescription: Master the primitives behind the cryptography APIs most developers only copy and paste.
MetaDescription: Learn cryptography in Go by building ciphers, hashes, password security, and encryption tools. Understand security beneath the library call.
Description: Ever wondered how your data stays safe online? This deep-dive in the Go programming language will teach you all about encryption, password security, ciphers, and more. After you master the fundamentals, you'll learn how each cryptographic primitive is used in real-world applications.

Chapters.Title: Symmetric Encryption
Chapters.Description: Learn the basics of cryptography: encrypting and decrypting a secret message with a key

Chapters.Title: Encoding
Chapters.Description: To be able to work with encrypted data, you need to understand how information is encoded in different formats

Chapters.Title: Brute Force
Chapters.Description: Learn about the most primitive, but most important kind of crypto attack

Chapters.Title: Caesar Cipher
Chapters.Description: Learn about the fundamentals of ciphers, starting with Julius Caesar's famous cipher

Chapters.Title: XOR
Chapters.Description: Study the importance of the XOR operation in cryptography and how it can be used to create the perfect cipher

Chapters.Title: Stream Ciphers
Chapters.Description: Stream ciphers can be used to encrypt data as it flows through a system

Chapters.Title: Block Ciphers
Chapters.Description: Block ciphers are commonly used to encrypt large amounts of data at rest, like entire hard drives

Chapters.Title: DES
Chapters.Description: DES is an older block cipher that's important to understand to appreciate modern block ciphers

Chapters.Title: AES
Chapters.Description: AES is a secure, modern block cipher that's used in many production applications

Chapters.Title: Asymmetric Encryption
Chapters.Description: Asymmetric encryption is used in HTTPS, digital signatures, and really anywhere two parties need to securely communicate

Chapters.Title: RSA
Chapters.Description: RSA is one of the most popular asymmetric encryption algorithms

Chapters.Title: Hash Functions
Chapters.Description: Hashing has many applications in cryptography, including password security and message integrity

Chapters.Title: Digital Signatures
Chapters.Description: Digital signatures are a way to prove that a message was sent by a particular party

Chapters.Title: KDFs
Chapters.Description: Key derivation functions are how we derive keys from passwords or from other keys securely

Title: Learn Data Structures and Algorithms 2 in Python
ShortDescription: The sequel is harder: graphs, dynamic programming, and the problem-solving reps interviews actually test.
MetaDescription: Advance your algorithms skills in Python with graphs, dynamic programming, linear programming, and tougher problem-solving practice.
Description: You might know algorithms, but do you know advanced algorithms? We're not really sure what that means either, because the distinction is a bit arbitrary. In a nutshell, this is an extension of our basic algorithms course, and here you'll learn more niche algorithms and pick up new ways of thinking that will help you advance your career from a junior to a senior developer.

Chapters.Title: Dijkstra's
Chapters.Description: Learn about Dijkstra's algorithm, one of the best ways to find the shortest path between two points

Chapters.Title: Bellman-Ford
Chapters.Description: Implement the Bellman-Ford algorithm, a specialized way to find the shortest path on a weighted graph

Chapters.Title: Heaps
Chapters.Description: Master priority queues and heaps. You'll efficiently find the minimum or maximum element in a collection.

Chapters.Title: A* Search
Chapters.Description: The A* algorithm is an advanced search that uses heuristics to guide the search to a more optimal solution

Chapters.Title: N Queens
Chapters.Description: Learn how to solve the classic N Queens problem with recursive backtracking, constraint checks, pruning, and complexity analysis.

Chapters.Title: Dynamic Programming
Chapters.Description: Dynamic programming is all about trading space for time. Learn how we can take exponential problems and make them linear.

Chapters.Title: Edit Distance
Chapters.Description: Edit distance is a classic problem in computer science; learn how to solve it with dynamic programming

Chapters.Title: Linear Programming
Chapters.Description: Learn about how to solve complex optimization problems

Title: Learn Retrieval Augmented Generation
ShortDescription: Build a RAG pipeline instead of just prompting harder: chunk data, search it, retrieve it, and feed it to an LLM.
MetaDescription: Learn Retrieval Augmented Generation in Python by building embeddings, chunking, search, retrieval, and an LLM-powered RAG pipeline.
Description: Learn all about modern search engine techniques like keyword, vector, semantic and LLM-enhanced search. In this course you'll implement different search techniques from scratch in Python everything from simple keyword search up to a fully functional Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using free models on OpenRouter (via the OpenAI SDK).

Chapters.Title: Preprocessing
Chapters.Description: Normalize and clean raw corpora so they are ready for indexing and downstream retrieval tasks.

Chapters.Title: TF-IDF
Chapters.Description: Construct inverted indexes and weighting schemes so keyword search can rank documents effectively.

Chapters.Title: Keyword Search
Chapters.Description: Tune keyword retrieval with BM25 refinements and metadata boosts to improve lexical relevance.

Chapters.Title: Semantic Search
Chapters.Description: Apply embeddings, similarity metrics, and vector databases to deliver semantic retrieval and RAG responses.

Chapters.Title: Chunking
Chapters.Description: Partition documents into context-preserving segments so RAG pipelines can retrieve the right snippets efficiently.

Chapters.Title: Hybrid Search
Chapters.Description: Blend lexical and semantic scores into unified retrieval pipelines that boost ranking quality.

Chapters.Title: LLMs
Chapters.Description: Leverage large language models to expand queries, correct intent, and orchestrate retrieval workflows.

Chapters.Title: Reranking
Chapters.Description: Re-score retrieved candidates with rerankers to surface the most relevant answers.

Chapters.Title: Evaluation
Chapters.Description: Measure retrieval precision, recall, and relevance so you can systematically improve RAG performance.

Chapters.Title: Augmented Generation
Chapters.Description: Combine retrieved context with LLMs to synthesize coherent, grounded answers for end users.

Chapters.Title: Agentic
Chapters.Description: Deploy autonomous agents that iteratively refine queries and navigate complex retrieval workflows.

Chapters.Title: Multimodal
Chapters.Description: Extend RAG to images and other modalities with multimodal embeddings and cross-modal retrieval.

Title: Learn File Servers and CDNs with S3 and CloudFront
ShortDescription: Store and serve user files like a Go backend developer: S3, CloudFront, streaming, and secure access.
MetaDescription: Learn file servers and CDNs with Go, AWS S3, and CloudFront. Upload, serve, stream, and secure files in backend systems.
Description: Learn how to integrate AWS S3 into your applications for scalable, secure, and durable file storage. This course covers the fundamentals of object storage, S3 bucket configuration, versioning, access control, presigned URLs, and more. Build a production-ready file storage system that handles large assets with ease.

Chapters.Title: File Storage
Chapters.Description: Learn the fundamentals of file storage, including handling large files, multipart uploads, encoding formats, and best practices for managing and serving files in modern backend systems.

Chapters.Title: Caching
Chapters.Description: Learn how browsers cache assets and how to ensure fresh content delivery.

Chapters.Title: AWS S3
Chapters.Description: Learn AWS S3 basics, from single-machine setups to serverless architectures and object uploads.

Chapters.Title: Object Storage
Chapters.Description: Discover the fundamentals of object storage, how S3 simulates directories, and managing dynamic file paths.

Chapters.Title: Video Streaming
Chapters.Description: Learn how to enable video streaming with S3, handle MP4 files effectively, and explore alternative approaches for adaptive and live streaming.

Chapters.Title: Security
Chapters.Description: Understand S3 security essentials, from scoping permissions and private buckets to signed URLs and encryption for secure file storage.

Chapters.Title: CDNs
Chapters.Description: Learn about Content Delivery Networks (CDNs), specifically AWS CloudFront and how they can help improve the performance and security of your app.

Chapters.Title: Resiliency
Chapters.Description: Explore S3's resiliency features, including availability, reliability, durability, versioning, regional replication, and object deletion strategies.

Title: Build a Web Scraper in TypeScript
ShortDescription: Build an SEO-flavored crawler in TypeScript that maps internal links and teaches you how scraping really works.
MetaDescription: Build a web scraper in TypeScript that crawls sites and reports internal links. Practice HTTP, HTML parsing, and real crawler logic.
Description: Use TypeScript to build a web crawler that scrapes pages. It's a tool that any SEO expert would be happy to have. You'll make HTTP requests and parse HTML to generate reports that can easily marshaled to standard output or a file. If you're interested in getting a job using Node.js, this project will teach you how Node can be used to build a command-line application.

Chapters.Title: Setup
Chapters.Description: Get started with web scraping by setting up your TypeScript environment and building essential functions to normalize URLs and extract links from HTML content.

Chapters.Title: Concurrency
Chapters.Description: Learn to speed up your web crawler using async/await and concurrency, crawling multiple pages simultaneously while respecting configurable limits.

Chapters.Title: Reporting
Chapters.Description: Learn to analyze and export your web scraping results with custom reporting tools that generate both human-readable summaries and JSON datasets for further analysis.

Title: Learn HTTP Servers in TypeScript
ShortDescription: Build the server side of the web: routes, middleware, auth, and APIs in TypeScript.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP servers in TypeScript by building routes, handlers, middleware, authentication, JSON APIs, and production-style backend patterns.
Description: Learn all about the basics of building web servers in TypeScript. We'll keep the stack lean and simple, using primarily Express.js. Once you've completed this course, you'll feel confident building your own web servers, whether you're serving static files or a full-on RESTful JSON API with authentication and authorization.

Chapters.Title: Servers
Chapters.Description: We'll cover the basics of web servers, and why TypeScript is such a great language for building web servers

Chapters.Title: Routing
Chapters.Description: We'll cover the basics of HTTP routing, and how to build a router using Express

Chapters.Title: Architecture
Chapters.Description: We'll talk about some different web architectures, and how to choose the right one for your project

Chapters.Title: JSON
Chapters.Description: Learn about how to parse and send JSON data from a TypeScript server

Chapters.Title: Error Handling
Chapters.Description: Learn about how to effectively handle errors in a TypeScript server

Chapters.Title: Storage
Chapters.Description: We'll add a PostgreSQL database and talk about data storage and database migrations

Chapters.Title: Authentication
Chapters.Description: We'll build an authentication system using JWTs from scratch, and compare JWTs to other authentication methods

Chapters.Title: Authorization
Chapters.Description: Learn about how authorization differs from authentication, and how to implement it in your application

Chapters.Title: Webhooks
Chapters.Description: Webhooks are a special kind of HTTP handler that you'll use often in modern web applications. Learn how to use them here

Chapters.Title: Documentation
Chapters.Description: Learn about documentation, why it's important, and learn about some tools that can help you write good documentation

Title: Learn AWS
ShortDescription: Get hands-on with AWS by deploying networks, servers, storage, and serverless apps using real cloud services.
MetaDescription: Learn AWS by deploying real cloud infrastructure. Practice IAM, EC2, networking, storage, and services developers use in production.
Description: Learn AWS and cloud computing from the ground up by building real infrastructure yourself. In this hands-on course, you'll cover cloud infrastructure basics like VPC networking, compute, storage, IAM, and monitoring, then move into deploying apps on AWS with services like EC2, ECS, Lambda, RDS, S3, Route 53, and CloudFront.

Chapters.Title: Cloud Computing
Chapters.Description: Build a foundation in cloud computing concepts, AWS accounts, regions, and cost models before provisioning infrastructure.

Chapters.Title: Networking - VPCs
Chapters.Description: Learn AWS networking basics by building VPCs, subnets, route tables, and internet access for secure app deployments.

Chapters.Title: EC2 - Elastic Compute Cloud
Chapters.Description: Deploy and manage virtual servers on AWS EC2, including security groups, scaling, and production deployment workflows.

Chapters.Title: RDS - Relational Database Service
Chapters.Description: Provision and operate managed PostgreSQL databases with AWS RDS for reliable, scalable application backends.

Chapters.Title: IAM - Identity and Access Management
Chapters.Description: Secure your AWS environment with IAM users, roles, and policies that enforce least-privilege access for deployed apps.

Chapters.Title: Monitoring - CloudWatch
Chapters.Description: Monitor AWS infrastructure and applications with CloudWatch metrics, logs, and alerts to maintain healthy production systems.

Chapters.Title: DNS – Route 53
Chapters.Description: Configure domains and DNS routing with Amazon Route 53 so users can reliably reach apps hosted on AWS.

Chapters.Title: S3 - Simple Storage Service
Chapters.Description: Store and serve application assets with Amazon S3 using buckets, object permissions, and production-ready storage patterns.

Chapters.Title: CDN – CloudFront
Chapters.Description: Speed up global delivery by placing CloudFront in front of AWS-hosted apps and static assets.

Chapters.Title: ECS - Elastic Container Service
Chapters.Description: Deploy containerized applications on AWS ECS with task definitions, load balancers, and scalable service orchestration.

Chapters.Title: Serverless Functions – Lambda
Chapters.Description: Build and deploy serverless applications on AWS Lambda and connect them to API Gateway for production APIs.

Title: Learn Data Visualization with Power BI
ShortDescription: Turn messy data into dashboards people can actually use to make decisions.
MetaDescription: Learn Power BI by turning raw data into interactive dashboards, reports, and business intelligence visuals for real decisions.
Description: In this interactive course, you'll master the fundamentals of data visualization using Microsoft Power BI, the industry-leading business intelligence tool. You'll learn how to connect to diverse data sources, clean and model complex datasets using Power Query and DAX, and create compelling interactive reports that tell a story. Whether you're a data analyst, business professional, or aspiring data scientist, you'll gain the hands-on experience needed to turn raw numbers into actionable business insights.

Chapters.Title: Data Sources
Chapters.Description: Learn to connect data sources and build your very first visualization in Power BI.

Chapters.Title: Basic Charts
Chapters.Description: Master the foundational building blocks of visualization, from bar charts to multi-line graphs.

Chapters.Title: Color
Chapters.Description: Learn to use color theory and conditional formatting to highlight what matters most.

Chapters.Title: Data Preparation
Chapters.Description: Clean, transform, and shape messy data using the Power Query Editor and custom scripts.

Chapters.Title: Data Modeling
Chapters.Description: Organize your data with star schemas, relationships, and measures for great reporting.

Chapters.Title: DAX
Chapters.Description: Unlock the power of Data Analysis Expressions (DAX) to model complex business logic.

Chapters.Title: More Charts
Chapters.Description: Expand your visualization toolkit with charts like gauges, area charts, and scatter plots.

Chapters.Title: Dashboards
Chapters.Description: Assemble your reports into cohesive, interactive dashboards that tell a clear story.

Chapters.Title: Collaboration
Chapters.Description: Publish your insights to the web and collaborate with team members in the Power BI Service.

Title: Build a Pokedex in TypeScript
ShortDescription: Build a command-line Pokedex with a REPL, API calls, JSON parsing, and caching in TypeScript.
MetaDescription: Build a command-line Pokedex in TypeScript with a REPL, HTTP requests, JSON parsing, caching, and API-backed game data.
Description: A REPL, or Read-Eval-Print Loop, is a simple interactive programming environment that takes user input, evaluates it, and returns the result to the user. In this guided project, you'll build a Pokedex-like REPL in TypeScript that uses the PokeAPI to fetch data about Pokemon. It's a great way to put your TypeScript knowledge to the test and learn valuable skills like HTTP networking and data serialization.

Chapters.Title: REPL
Chapters.Description: A REPL (Read, Eval, Print Loop) is a simple interactive programming environment that takes user inputs, evaluates them, and returns the results. Learn how to implement one from scratch in TypeScript.

Chapters.Title: Cache
Chapters.Description: When making network requests, one of the most common performance optimizations is to cache the results. Implement an in-memory cache for the PokeAPI in TypeScript.

Chapters.Title: Pokedex
Chapters.Description: Bring your REPL and cache together to complete your fully functional Pokedex CLI.

Title: Learn Pub/Sub in RabbitMQ and TypeScript
ShortDescription: Build event-driven backend flows with RabbitMQ, not just another request-response API.
MetaDescription: Learn Pub/Sub with RabbitMQ and TypeScript. Build producers, consumers, queues, exchanges, and event-driven backend workflows.
Description: Learn how to build large-scale asynchronous Pub/Sub architectures using RabbitMQ and TypeScript. Understand how pub/sub systems differ from point-to-point systems, and how to use real-world technologies to make them work. Write hands-on real-time applications that use a local RabbitMQ server to communicate between services.

Chapters.Title: Pub/Sub Architecture
Chapters.Description: Understand the basics of Pub/Sub architecture and how it differs from point-to-point systems.

Chapters.Title: Message Brokers
Chapters.Description: Understand the role of message brokers in pub/sub systems and which are the most popular options.

Chapters.Title: Publishers & Queues
Chapters.Description: Use publishers and queues to send and receive messages.

Chapters.Title: Subscribers & Routing
Chapters.Description: Learn how to route messages to different subscribers and use advanced routing patterns.

Chapters.Title: Delivery
Chapters.Description: Build robust systems that can handle message delivery failures and ensure messages make it to their destination.

Chapters.Title: Serialization
Chapters.Description: Learn how to serialize and deserialize messages and handle different message formats.

Chapters.Title: Scalability
Chapters.Description: Learn how to handle large-scale systems without sacrificing performance.

Title: Second Personal Project
ShortDescription: Take another swing at independent project work with more judgment, less hand-holding, and a stronger portfolio target.
MetaDescription: Build a second personal programming project with more independence. Strengthen your portfolio by planning, coding, debugging, and shipping real software.
Description: You can't truly become a developer if you can't build your own stuff. Theory and foundations are important, but you also need to be able to fly solo. The purpose of this project is two-fold: a chance to put your skills into practice and to add another project to your portfolio. Well-built personal projects will help you land interviews and jobs, so make sure to put in the effort!

Chapters.Title: Placeholder
Chapters.Description: Placeholder

Title: Learn HTTP Clients in Python
ShortDescription: Call real APIs, parse JSON, handle errors, and make HTTP feel boring in Python.
MetaDescription: Learn HTTP clients in Python by making API requests, parsing JSON, handling errors, authentication, and working with real web data.
Description: Learn the backbone of the modern web: HTTP network communications. You will be writing real HTTP requests in Python, then parsing and displaying the responses. Each challenge in this course is part of a larger application so that you will understand how HTTP is used in the real world.

Chapters.Title: Why HTTP?
Chapters.Description: Learn about HTTP and how it powers the majority of web communication

Chapters.Title: DNS
Chapters.Description: Learn about domain names and how they map human-readable names to physical servers

Chapters.Title: URIs
Chapters.Description: Understand URIs and URLs at a deeper level

Chapters.Title: Async
Chapters.Description: Practice writing asynchronous Python, get a feel for concurrent programming

Chapters.Title: Headers
Chapters.Description: Practice adding custom headers to HTTP requests and learn about authorization

Chapters.Title: JSON
Chapters.Description: Learn one of the most popular data formatting notations in the programming world

Chapters.Title: Methods
Chapters.Description: Learn about GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests

Chapters.Title: Paths
Chapters.Description: Learn how URL paths and query parameters relate to typical HTTP requests

Chapters.Title: HTTPS
Chapters.Description: Learn how to encrypt HTTP requests and keep them secure

Title: Learn CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Docker and TypeScript
ShortDescription: Take a working TypeScript app and automate the boring, breakable parts: tests, builds, containers, and deploys.
MetaDescription: Learn CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Docker for TypeScript apps. Automate tests, builds, containers, and cloud deployments.
Description: You'll build and test a real codebase, and then automate that same build process to deploy an application to the cloud. We'll cover technologies like GitHub Actions, Docker, GCP, Cloud Run, and Turso. If you want to learn how modern tech companies test and ship products, this course is for you.

Chapters.Title: Continuous Integration
Chapters.Description: Learn the basics of CI with GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Tests
Chapters.Description: Learn how to write and run tests both locally and with GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Formatting
Chapters.Description: Learn how to run automated code format checks in the cloud

Chapters.Title: Linting
Chapters.Description: Learn how to run automated code linting checks and learn about the difference between linting and formatting

Chapters.Title: Security
Chapters.Description: Learn about automatic security checks and how to set them up in a TypeScript project

Chapters.Title: Build
Chapters.Description: Learn how to build a TypeScript project in the cloud and Dockerize it in GitHub Actions

Chapters.Title: Deploy
Chapters.Description: Learn how to deploy a TypeScript project to the public internet using GCP and Cloud Run

Chapters.Title: Database
Chapters.Description: Learn how to connect your public Node service to a managed Turso database. Run migrations in continuous delivery pipelines

Title: Learn Logging and Observability in Go
ShortDescription: Add the logs, metrics, traces, and alerts you'll wish you had when production catches fire.
MetaDescription: Learn observability in Go by adding structured logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, and alerts to production-style web services.
Description: Build production-ready logging and observability skills for Go applications. You'll design structured, context-rich logs, collect and visualize metrics with Prometheus and Grafana, configure actionable alerts, and profile performance with pprof. By the end, you'll be able to instrument distributed traces with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger to debug failures and latency bottlenecks fast.

Chapters.Title: Observability
Chapters.Description: Understand observability fundamentals and the pillars of logs, metrics, and traces so you can debug Go services with confidence.

Chapters.Title: Logging
Chapters.Description: Implement reliable logging in Go using proper logger setup, dependency injection, buffering, and lifecycle management.

Chapters.Title: Structured Logging
Chapters.Description: Use Go's slog package to produce structured logs with levels, key-value fields, and output formats that scale in production.

Chapters.Title: Log Strategies
Chapters.Description: Apply logging best practices that reduce noise, preserve signal, and make production incidents easier to investigate.

Chapters.Title: Logging Errors
Chapters.Description: Capture error logs effectively with stack traces, grouped context, and rich attributes while avoiding duplicate or misleading logs.

Chapters.Title: Logging Context
Chapters.Description: Attach request, user, build, and instance context to logs so every event is searchable, correlated, and actionable.

Chapters.Title: Log Storage
Chapters.Description: Route logs to console, files, and syslog with rotation strategies that keep long-running services observable and maintainable.

Chapters.Title: Log Security
Chapters.Description: Protect sensitive data in logs using filtering, obfuscation, encryption, and safer error-response logging patterns.

Chapters.Title: Metrics
Chapters.Description: Instrument Go services with Prometheus and Grafana, collect system and app metrics, and build dashboards that reveal health trends.

Chapters.Title: Alerting
Chapters.Description: Design actionable alerts with sensible thresholds to catch real issues early without creating alert fatigue.

Chapters.Title: Profiling
Chapters.Description: Use pprof to analyze CPU, memory, and goroutine behavior in Go applications and fix performance bottlenecks.

Chapters.Title: Tracing
Chapters.Description: Instrument distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger to follow requests across services and diagnose latency issues.

Title: Build a Web Scraper in Go
ShortDescription: Build an SEO-flavored crawler in Go that maps internal links and teaches you how scraping really works.
