MetaDescription: Build a web scraper in Go that crawls sites and reports internal links. Practice HTTP, HTML parsing, and real crawler logic.
Description: Use Go to build a web crawler that scrapes pages. It's a tool that any SEO expert would be happy to have. You'll make HTTP requests and parse HTML to generate reports that can easily marshaled to standard output or a file. If you're interested in getting a job doing data analysis, this project will teach you how Go can be used to build a command-line application.

Chapters.Title: Setup
Chapters.Description: Setup the environment.

Chapters.Title: Crawling
Chapters.Description: Build the core crawling engine by implementing the main crawler logic, HTTP request handling, and recursive page discovery to systematically traverse websites.

Chapters.Title: Concurrency
Chapters.Description: Learn to speed up your web crawler using goroutines and concurrency, crawling multiple pages simultaneously while respecting configurable limits.

Chapters.Title: Reporting
Chapters.Description: Learn to analyze and export your web scraping results with custom reporting tools that generate both human-readable summaries and JSON datasets for further analysis.

Title: Build a Blog Aggregator in TypeScript
ShortDescription: Build an RSS-powered TypeScript backend with a database, background work, and a real API shape.
MetaDescription: Build a Blog Aggregator in TypeScript with PostgreSQL, RSS feeds, background jobs, and CLI tooling for a real backend project.
Description: In this guided project you'll practice building a CLI in TypeScript, and you'll use production-ready database tools like PostgreSQL and Drizzle. This won't just be another CLI utility, but a service that has a long-running service worker that reaches out over the internet to fetch data from remote locations.

Chapters.Title: Config
Chapters.Description: Build a system for the CLI tool that allows users to get and set configuration values

Chapters.Title: Database
Chapters.Description: Set up Postgres and drizzle to allow your CLI application to store and retrieve data.

Chapters.Title: RSS
Chapters.Description: Build functions that will download and parse data from RSS feeds

Chapters.Title: Following
Chapters.Description: Add to the multiplayer features of the project by allowing users to follow other RSS feeds

Chapters.Title: Aggregate
Chapters.Description: Turn your CLI into a long running service that continously aggregates posts from RSS feeds.

Title: Learn File Servers and CDNs with S3 and CloudFront
ShortDescription: Store and serve user files like a TypeScript backend developer: S3, CloudFront, streaming, and secure access.
MetaDescription: Learn file servers and CDNs with TypeScript, AWS S3, and CloudFront. Upload, serve, stream, and secure files in backend systems.
Description: Learn how to integrate AWS S3 into your applications for scalable, secure, and durable file storage. This course covers the fundamentals of object storage, S3 bucket configuration, versioning, access control, presigned URLs, and more. Build a production-ready file storage system that handles large assets with ease.

Chapters.Title: File Storage
Chapters.Description: Learn the fundamentals of file storage, including handling large files, multipart uploads, encoding formats, and best practices for managing and serving files in modern backend systems.

Chapters.Title: Caching
Chapters.Description: Learn how browsers cache assets and how to ensure fresh content delivery.

Chapters.Title: AWS S3
Chapters.Description: Learn AWS S3 basics, from single-machine setups to serverless architectures and object uploads.

Chapters.Title: Object Storage
Chapters.Description: Discover the fundamentals of object storage, how S3 simulates directories, and managing dynamic file paths.

Chapters.Title: Video Streaming
Chapters.Description: Learn how to enable video streaming with S3, handle MP4 files effectively, and explore alternative approaches for adaptive and live streaming.

Chapters.Title: Security
Chapters.Description: Understand S3 security essentials, from scoping permissions and private buckets to signed URLs and encryption for secure file storage.

Chapters.Title: CDNs
Chapters.Description: Learn about Content Delivery Networks (CDNs), specifically AWS CloudFront and how they can help improve the performance and security of your app.

Chapters.Title: Resiliency
Chapters.Description: Explore S3's resiliency features, including availability, reliability, durability, versioning, regional replication, and object deletion strategies.

Title: Build a Web Scraper in Python
ShortDescription: Build an SEO-flavored crawler in Python that maps internal links and teaches you how scraping really works.
MetaDescription: Build a web scraper in Python that crawls sites and reports internal links. Practice HTTP, HTML parsing, and real crawler logic.
Description: Use Python to build a web crawler that scrapes pages. It's a tool that any SEO expert would be happy to have. You'll make HTTP requests and parse HTML to generate reports that can easily marshaled to standard output or a file. If you're interested in getting a job doing data analysis, this project will teach you how Python can be used to build a command-line application.

Chapters.Title: Setup
Chapters.Description: Get started with web scraping by setting up your Python environment and building essential functions to normalize URLs and extract links from HTML content.

Chapters.Title: Crawling
Chapters.Description: Build the core crawling engine by implementing the main crawler logic, HTTP request handling, and recursive page discovery to systematically traverse websites.

Chapters.Title: Concurrency
Chapters.Description: Learn to speed up your web crawler using asynchronous programming and concurrency, crawling multiple pages simultaneously while respecting configurable limits.

Chapters.Title: Reporting
Chapters.Description: Learn to analyze and export your web scraping results with custom reporting tools that generate both human-readable summaries and JSON datasets for further analysis.
